column64:
Information-Service Modality (CE-A): The customer wanted to update their registered SIM information from an old NID to a Smart NID. The CM (Case Manager / Agent) incorrectly advised the customer that they could complete this update directly through live chat. However, as per the standard service modality for Individual to Individual TOF / ID Swap (NID to SNID) for the same customer, this process cannot be done online and strictly requires:

1.Physical Presence of the customer at a service center.

2.A Valid NEW ID (Original) (Note: For NID/Smart ID verification, a photocopy or soft copy is allowed during the physical visit).

column64: Customer inquired whether it was possible to reserve or retrieve previously expired data volume. However, CM informed the customer that whenever any MB or minutes expire, the customer can repurchase the same offer and contact us within 15 days. CM further stated that a request would then be raised, and the previously expired volume would be restored. This information was incorrect. As per the prescribed process, expired volume can only be considered for restoration in cases where the customer experiences a portfolio change and the existing carry-forward volume is not transferred to the new portfolio. In such situations, if the customer purchases a new eligible offer and contacts us within 15 days after the expiry of the newly purchased offer, a complaint can be raised under Positive CEX, subject to approval, to restore the lapsed volume.
